title: Files (Q&A)
description: Understanding file types, cuts, safety, and filing techniques.

questions.prompt: Match the correct file name to its cross-section image.
questions.exampleContext: Different jobs require different profiles, such as getting into a sharp corner versus smoothing a curved slot. Why are files manufactured with various cross-sections?

questions.prompt: Describe the difference between single and double cut files.
questions.exampleContext: Some files are meant for heavy material removal, while others are for a fine finish. What is the physical difference in the teeth between a single and double cut file?

questions.prompt: Review the image of the file. List three checks you would make to ensure it is safe to use.
questions.exampleContext: Before using any hand tool, it must be inspected. What specific parts of a file, especially the handle area, must be checked to ensure it is safe to use?

questions.prompt: Describe how you would prepare material to be filed.
questions.exampleContext: Filing an unsecured piece of metal is dangerous and inaccurate. How should you prepare and secure your workpiece before you begin filing?

questions.prompt: Explain what soft jaws / vice jaw plates are used for and the consequences of not using them.
questions.exampleContext: Clamping a finished aluminum part in a steel vice will crush and scratch it. What accessories should you use in the vice to protect delicate workpieces?

questions.prompt: State the tool and technique required to achieve a flat surface.
questions.exampleContext: Filing a perfectly flat surface by hand requires a specific technique. What motion and grip should you use to avoid creating a curved surface?

questions.prompt: State the tool and technique required to achieve a smooth surface finish.
questions.exampleContext: Once the material is flat, you may want to remove the rough file marks. What specific filing technique is used to achieve a very smooth, polished finish?
